danger_signals.visual_form: Corrupt police officers and Morph creatures rendered with rotoscoped animation
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to Conrad; areas with hostile entities require evasion or combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Multi-level screen layouts with significant vertical drops
danger_signals.meaning: Conrad dies from falls; spatial layout itself is a danger indicator

danger_signals.visual_form: Force shield visual degradation (absorbed shots reduce shield capacity)
danger_signals.meaning: Declining protection status; player is under active fire threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Humanoid enemies disguised within society; revealed through context and behavior
danger_signals.meaning: Invisible threat embedded in world; authority figures may be compromised

safety_signals.visual_form: Clearly defined, lighter-toned platforms within multi-level screens
safety_signals.meaning: Navigable safe zones; areas Conrad can reach without damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty corridors and outdoor areas in the jungle and city
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary respite; exploration zone free of immediate threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Ian and other human allies rendered distinctly from enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction point; narrative advancement and utility (regenerator device)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Corrupt police officers depicted as enemies attacking Ian; authority figures rendered as antagonists
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: New Washington city infrastructure; official buildings and checkpoints

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Alien species; implied visual distinction from humans (identity concealed in-world)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Humanoid forms disguised as authority; revealed through context and gameplay mechanics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hideout beneath nightclub; futuristic alien technology; teleportation devices
Safe houses and meeting points in New Washington; locations providing utility items and memory restoration

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Conrad has amnesia at game start
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Disoriented movement in jungle; limited environmental recognition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must explore and uncover memories through found recordings and NPC interaction

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Society is infiltrated by Morphs (unknown to most citizens)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Normal-looking city and authority figures rendered identically to legitimate humans; deception is visual ambiguity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Police attack without provocation; legitimate authority acts as enemy; player must navigate distrust of established power

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Flashback sequences and visions integrated into gameplay; Conrad gains context and purpose
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat and mission objectives become clear; player gains agency to pursue Morph discovery

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Danger escalates as Conrad approaches truth
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progression from city environments to alien hideout; increasingly hostile enemies; advanced alien technology visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Levels become spatially complex; enemy density increases; final confrontation with Morphs and their home planet access

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pistol, force shield, and portable force field rendered as on-screen UI elements and equipment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat becomes viable; shield absorbs damage visually; later acquisition of teleportation device enables spatial puzzle solutions

environmental_storytelling.technique: Multi-level screen design communicates spatial complexity
environmental_storytelling.example: Each level spans many non-scrolling screens with multiple altitudes; platforms and ledges telegraph danger and safe paths simultaneously

environmental_storytelling.technique: Contextual enemy placement reveals political structure
environmental_storytelling.example: Corrupt police in city areas establish government as threat; Morphs in hideout reveal alien presence beneath surface power structures

environmental_storytelling.technique: Found recordings and memory fragments advance narrative and world-state
environmental_storytelling.example: Conrad's own recording in jungle jumpstarts quest; memory restoration via regenerator device reveals Morph conspiracy

environmental_storytelling.technique: Architecture and technology scale telegraph progression
environmental_storytelling.example: Jungle and New Washington are familiar human environments; Morph hideout beneath nightclub and alien planet represent escalating stakes

environmental_storytelling.technique: Rotoscoped animation provides realistic human physicality
environmental_storytelling.example: Conrad's realistic running speed, jumping ability, and vulnerability to falls communicate him as mortal human protagonist; enemy animations reveal threat level

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active agency; pursues objectives; combat capable; explores and solves spatial puzzles
Passive exploration; follows recorded guidance; dependent on NPC aid for memory restoration
Patrol and attack without reason; establish illegitimacy of authority; require evasion or combat
Disguised as humans; organize from hideout; only become visible threats in final levels; employ advanced technology
Provides utility (regenerator device); offers exposition; establishes path to escape Titan

notes: Flashback uses cinematic platformer design to communicate world-state through visual hierarchy and spatial layout rather than explicit UI elements. The game's central narrative conceit—hidden alien infiltration—is reflected in visual ambiguity: Morphs are indistinguishable from humans until narrative context reveals them as threats. The progression from jungle to city to alien hideout mirrors Conrad's mental and situational awakening. Rotoscoped animation technique provides realistic human movement, making environmental hazards (falls, combat) feel viscerally dangerous. The contrast between civilian spaces and militarized hideouts communicates power dynamics and conspiracy visually.
